       INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : BEST PRACTICES FOR TESTING AND ROLLING OUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION




Internet & SaaS (ZIA)
Best Practices for Testing and Rolling Out
SSL/TLS Inspection
As a best practice, Zscaler recommends that you enable SSL/TLS Inspection in a small location or test
lab before enabling it on all locations in your organization. This allows you to test your deployment of
SSL/TLS Inspection with a select number of users.
To enable SSL/TLS Inspection, first deploy the Zscaler or custom intermediate root certificate. Then,
enable SSL/TLS Inspection for the location or sublocation you are using for testing.

Testing SSL/TLS Inspection
Before testing SSL/TLS Inspection, define the set of users you want to use for testing. For example, you
can choose users from the IT department, such as application authors and owners, support staff
members, proxy team members, or security team members. You can also choose managers and end
users from non-IT departments.
To test SSL/TLS Inspection:
  1. Compile a list of the websites and applications that your organization uses for everyday
     operations. Remember to include vendor sites and applications.
  2. Enable SSL/TLS Inspection for the websites and applications from the list by configuring the
     SSL/TLS Inspection policy, and then have the users test them.

         When you are configuring the SSL/TLS Inspection policy, you are specifying the URL
         categories or applications that you do not want the service to inspect. For example, if you
         want to enable SSL/TLS Inspection for the Legal Liability categories only, then select the
        Do Not Inspect action for all the URL categories except the Legal Liability categories in
         your SSL/TLS Inspection rule. The Zscaler service does not perform SSL/TLS Inspection
         on the specified URL categories, but does perform SSL/TLS Inspection on the Legal
         Liability categories.

  3. You might need to exempt some sites for SSL/TLS Inspection permanently, or you might need to
     report sites to Zscaler Support to identify the cause of failure.




                                                                             ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   1
    INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : BEST PRACTICES FOR TESTING AND ROLLING OUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION




      Zscaler recommends not to exempt the Miscellaneous or Unknown category for SSL/TLS
      Inspection if the traffic forwarded to Zscaler uses transparent proxy mode. In transparent
      mode, policies on both SNI and IP addresses are evaluated for exemption from SSL/TLS
      Inspection. Exempting SSL/TLS Inspection for the Miscellaneous or Unknown category
     exempts almost all traffic from SSL/TLS Inspection as most IP addresses are categorized
      as miscellaneous or unknown. However, in the case of explicit traffic forwarding, Zscaler
      evaluates the policies only based on SNI and does not exempt traffic from SSL/TLS
      Inspection based on IP address categories. To learn more, see Choosing Traffic
      Forwarding Methods.

4. After testing the list of websites and applications, test SSL/TLS Inspection for the URL categories.
   As a best practice, Zscaler recommends that you enable SSL/TLS Inspection for only certain URL
   categories at a time, and include the rest of the categories in the list of URL categories for which
   SSL/TLS transactions are not to be decrypted. Then, when your organization is ready, enable
   SSL/TLS Inspection for all URL categories.

      Some organizations choose to bypass SSL/TLS Inspection for Finance and Health URL
     categories due to privacy concerns. Zscaler recommends that you enable SSL/TLS
      Inspection for all URL categories.

  Enable and test SSL/TLS Inspection for the URL categories in the following order of phases:
      URL Categories in Phase 1


                                                    Phase 1
         Adult Themes
         Alcohol/Tobacco
         Anonymizer
         Computer Hacking
         Copyright Infringement
         Drugs
         Gambling
         Mature Humor
         Militancy/Hate and Extremism

                                                                           ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   2
INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : BEST PRACTICES FOR TESTING AND ROLLING OUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION




   Newly Registered and Observed Domains
   Non-Categorizable
   Nudity
   Other Adult Material
   Other Illegal or Questionable
   Other Security
   Peer-to-Peer Site
   Pornography
   Questionable
   Social Networking Adult
   Spyware/Adware
   Tasteless
   Violence
   Weapons/Bombs
   Blogs
   Classifieds
   Custom Encrypted Content
   Discussion Forums
   DNS Over HTTPS Services
   Dynamic DNS Host
   File Convertors
   FileHost
   General AI and ML Applications
   Generative AI and ML Applications
   Image Host
   Internet Services


                                                         ©2026 Zscaler, Inc. All rights reserved.   3
  INTERNET & SAAS (ZIA) : BEST PRACTICES FOR TESTING AND ROLLING OUT SSL/TLS INSPECTION




      Lifestyle
      Marijuana
      Military
      Miscellaneous or Unknown
      Newly Revived Domains
      News and Media
      Online and Other Games
      Online Chat
      Other Drugs
      Other Miscellaneous
      Portals
      Profanity
      Radio
      Remote Access Tools
      Safe Search Engine
      Shareware Download
      Streaming Media
      Web Search
      Webmail


Before testing the URL categories in phase 2, remember to keep SSL/TLS inspection enabled for
the URL categories in phase 1.
    URL Categories in Phase 2


                                              Phase 2
      Adult Sex Education
      Alt or New Age
      Alternate Lifestyle

   Body Art
   Continuing Education/Colleges

   Dining and Restaurant

   Family Issues
   Government
   History
   Hobbies and Leisure
   Job/Employment Search
   K-12
   K-12 Sex Education
   Lingerie/Bikini
   Music
   Online Auctions
   Online Shopping
   Other Education
   Other Entertainment/Recreation

   Other Internet Communication

   Other Shopping and Auctions

      Other Social and Family Issues
      Other Society and Lifestyle
      Politics
      Radio Stations
      Real Estate
      Reference Sites
      Science/Technology
      Social Issues
      Social Networking
      Social Networking Games
      Special Interests/Social Organizations
      Sports
      Television/Movies
      Traditional Religion
      Translators
      Travel
      Vehicles
      Online Trading and Brokerage
      Other Business and Economy
      Professional Services
      Video Streaming
      Web Conferencing
      Web Host


Before testing the URL categories in phase 3, remember to keep SSL/TLS inspection enabled for
the URL categories in phases 1 and 2.
    URL Categories in Phase 3
